name: Ensure unencrypted LDAP port (389) is not exposed to the internet
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when an AWS security group allows inbound TCP/UDP port 389 from `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`. LDAP on port 389 is unencrypted by default and should not be reachable from the internet.

Use LDAPS on port 636 or private network access instead.
description: AWS security groups that expose unencrypted LDAP port 389 to the internet.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Remove internet-facing inbound access to LDAP port 389.

1. Open the affected security group in **EC2 > Security Groups**.
2. Review inbound rules for port 389.
3. Delete any rule with source `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
4. If LDAP is required, restrict source CIDRs to trusted private networks or migrate to LDAPS on port 636.
5. Re-run sync and assessment.

AWS CLI examples:

```bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id {{asset.idFromProvider}} --protocol tcp --port 389 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 --region {{asset.region}}
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id {{asset.idFromProvider}} --ip-permissions 'IpProtocol=tcp,FromPort=389,ToPort=389,Ipv6Ranges=[{CidrIpv6=::/0}]' --region {{asset.region}}
```

Cyscale will pass when the security group no longer has inbound LDAP 389 open to the internet.
